description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: test-python, build-docs, and pypi. Add three unit-test jobs named python3_10_unit_test, python3_11_unit_test and python3_12_unit_test in the test-python stage, using the python:3.10-slim-bullseye, python:3.11-slim-bullseye and python:3.12-slim-bullseye Docker images respectively, each running apt-get update && apt-get install -y git, exporting PYTHONPATH=$PYTHONPATH:$CI_PROJECT_DIR, pip install -r requirements_test.txt, coverage run runtests.py, coverage report -m, coverage html, and publishing the htmlcov/ directory as an artifact with a coverage regex /TOTAL.*\s+(\d+\%)/. Add a pages job in the build-docs stage using the seriallab/python3.6dev image, limited to tag pipelines, that runs pip install -r requirements_test.txt, pip install sphinx sphinx_rtd_theme pygments ipython recommonmark, make docs, moves docs/_build/html/ to public/, and stores the public/ folder as a one-day-expiring artifact. Add a deploy_pypi job in the pypi stage also using seriallab/python3.6dev and only on tags, which writes a .pypirc file with the PYPI_USER and PYPI_PASSWORD variables, executes python setup.py check sdist bdist_wheel upload, then removes the .pypirc file, and defines an environment named deployment pointing to https://pypi.org/. The pipeline uses no global caches or services.
